transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you for calling Sunrise Recovery Center, this is Brandon. How can I help you today?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Hi... my name is Rebecca. I've been struggling with alcohol for a couple of years now and I think — I think I finally need to get some real help.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you so much for reaching out, Rebecca. I can hear that took a lot of courage. You don't have to figure this out alone — that's exactly why we're here. Can you tell me a little about what's been going on?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: It's been getting worse. I'm drinking every day, sometimes starting in the morning. My wife is scared and honestly so am I.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I really appreciate you sharing that with me. What you're describing — recognising the pattern and being scared — that actually tells me a lot about how serious you are about getting better. That's a strength, Rebecca. Let me ask — are you having any physical symptoms right now? Shaking, sweating, anything like that?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Not right now. I had my last drink a few hours ago.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Okay, good to know. So no immediate withdrawal symptoms at this moment. And is this something you're looking to address soon, or are you in a place where today or this week feels more urgent?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: I'd like to start as soon as possible. My wife and I talked last night and we agreed I need to do this now.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I'm glad you have that support at home — that makes a real difference. Let me get a few more details so we can figure out the right level of care for you. How long has the daily drinking been going on?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: About two years, maybe a bit more. It got a lot worse after I lost my job eighteen months ago.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I understand. And have you ever been through any treatment before — detox, rehab, outpatient, anything like that?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: I did an IOP program about four years ago. It helped for a while but I eventually went back.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's really useful context. Prior treatment history helps our clinical team put together the right plan. Now let me ask about insurance — do you have coverage right now?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes, through my wife's employer. It's BluePeak Health.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Great. Do you happen to have the insurance card handy? I'll need the member ID and group number.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Sure, hold on. Member ID is BP12132605 and the group number is GRP-337060.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect, thank you. So BluePeak Health, member ID BP12132605, group GRP-337060. Let me note that down. Our admissions coordinator will verify your benefits — typically we can get that back to you within a couple of hours. Have you or your wife had any questions about cost or what coverage looks like?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: A little. We weren't sure what insurance would cover.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Totally understandable. Once we verify, we'll walk you through exactly what your plan covers and what, if anything, would be your responsibility. We never want cost to be the thing that gets in the way. There are also financing options if needed, but let's see what the verification shows first.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: That's reassuring. Thank you.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Of course. Rebecca, based on what you've shared — two-year history, daily use, prior IOP — our clinical team will likely want to do a full assessment to determine whether detox is the right starting point or whether an IOP or PHP level of care fits best. Does that make sense?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes, that makes sense. I just want to do this right this time.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: And you will. Here's what happens next: I'm going to send you a confirmation email right now with our address, what to expect, and a short intake form. Our benefits coordinator will call you within two hours with your insurance verification results. Can you take a call this afternoon?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes, absolutely.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect. You'll hear from us by three o'clock today. And Rebecca — reaching out today was the right call. We'll take it from here.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Thank you. I really appreciate it.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Of course. Take care, and we'll talk soon.

gold_labels.coaching_notes: Exemplary across all scored dimensions. Agent used caller's name consistently, completed full clinical screen, and gave a precise next-step with timing. Minor: could have explicitly named the IOP/PHP distinction when describing level-of-care options.
